title: Well-Formed Formula
statement:
The set \(\WFF\) of well-formed formulas is the smallest set of strings satisfying the following formation rules:
- Every propositional variable \(P\in\Prop\) belongs to \(\WFF\).
- If \(\varphi\in\WFF\), then \(\neg\varphi\in\WFF\).
- If \(\varphi,\psi\in\WFF\) and \(\circ\in\{\land,\lor,\to,\leftrightarrow\}\), then \((\varphi\circ\psi)\in\WFF\).
- No string is a well-formed formula unless it is obtained by finitely many applications of the previous clauses.
